candidates.name: clangd-wasm
candidates.notes: Language-server only; no codegen pipeline. Detailed reason: clangd is the Language Server Protocol front-end of LLVM/Clang — it provides diagnostics, code completion, hover, go-to-definition, and similar editor intelligence features. It does NOT invoke the LLVM backend for code generation and produces no object files, WASM modules, or executable binaries. The best-maintained browser port (guyutongxue/clangd-in-browser) explicitly confirms this: its README states the project gives 'C++ IntelliSense directly without installing any native compiler', and its demo integrates only with Monaco Editor's language-client stack. The upstream clangd/clangd repository is equally clear that clangd is a language server, not a compiler. Browser projects that DO compile C++ to runnable WASM (binji/wasm-clang, tbfleming/cib) compile the full Clang + LLD toolchain to WASM — they use clang, not clangd — and both are unmaintained alpha/demo projects predating 2020. No actively-maintained clangd distribution that performs code generation was found.

candidates.name: jscpp
candidates.notes: Pure JS interpreter, no codegen. <bits/stdc++.h> almost certainly unsupported — included as fallback only.

candidates.name: binji-wasm-clang
candidates.notes: Live demo at https://binji.github.io/wasm-clang/ returns HTTP 200 with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on all artifacts (verified May 2026). shared.js exposes a full API class; worker.js wraps it with a compileLinkRun(sourceString) postMessage protocol — fully programmatic, no UI automation required. Payload: ~60 MB cold (clang ~31 MB + lld ~19 MB + sysroot.tar ~9 MB + memfs ~338 KB + JS glue). Repo updated as recently as May 2026 despite 'unmaintained' label. Key caveat: sysroot uses LLVM libc++, not libstdc++, so <bits/stdc++.h> is unavailable; driver uses sample-libc++.cpp with explicit includes instead — supportsBitsStdcpp is false. To measure: serve spike/wasm-toolchain/ with any static server (no special headers), open /binji/, wait for ~60 MB download, then click Cold compile then Warm compile. Note: binji's API has no explicit init() — the first compileLinkRun triggers all downloads; cold total (download + compile + link + run) is recorded as coldCompileMs. initMs is 0 until a browser run separates download from compile time.
